newsSince: No new fundamental information since June 9. The >$4B 2027-2029 AI data-center cooling agreement (with $165M upfront) and the Q4 FY26 results (sales +47%, FY26 adj. EPS $5.02, ~5-year visibility) were early-June and already in the prior case. Stock roughly flat (+1.0%) since June 9.

sources.label: Simply Wall St: Modine $4B AI cooling deal

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha lens: nothing new since June 9. The $4B backlog and Q4 beat were already chased; +100% YTD with multiple PT raises keeps it a fully rerated, crowded benchmark, not residual alpha. Hold in the rerated-benchmark bucket.
verdicts.bull: $4B contracted cooling backlog and ~5-year visibility; chiller capacity ramping (~20 lines by FY28) on hyperscale demand.
verdicts.neutral: Strategically important but the rerate has happened; crowded, well-covered name.
verdicts.bear: +100% YTD prices in the cooling boom; execution on capacity ramp must deliver.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Incremental hyperscale cooling orders and chiller-line ramp

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent lens: direct data-center cooling leverage but the market now recognizes it, reducing hiddenness. No new fundamentals since June 9 (the $4B deal is prior). Alpha now depends on execution and whether cooling stays undersupplied. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Clear AI-infrastructure cooling leverage with multi-year contracted demand and long-term customer agreements.
verdicts.neutral: Relevance high but hiddenness low post-rerate; execution-dependent.
verdicts.bear: Well-recognized story; latent-alpha edge largely gone.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Long-term cooling agreements and capacity execution
